name: Hasan al-Muthanna
title: Wounded Relic
shortDescription: The son of Imam Hasan (AS) who fought bravely in Karbala, was severely wounded, and survived through his uncle's intercession to continue Imam Hasan's lineage.
fullStory:
Hasan ibn Hasan (AS), known as Hasan al-Muthanna, was the son of Imam Hasan al-Mujtaba (AS) and the husband of Fatima bint Hussain (AS). He was a brave, learned, and pious youth who came to Karbala with his uncle, Imam Hussain (AS).

On the day of Ashura, Hasan al-Muthanna entered the battlefield with Haidari bravery and fought Umar Sa'd's army shoulder to shoulder with other Hashemite youths. After a long and heroic battle, he was severely wounded by numerous spear and sword strikes and fell among the fallen bodies.

After the battle ended, when the Kufans came to sever the heads of the martyrs, they noticed that Hasan al-Muthanna was still alive but severely weakened. At this moment, Asma' ibn Kharija al-Fazari, his maternal uncle, asked Umar Sa'd to spare his nephew.

Through his intercession, Hasan al-Muthanna was saved and taken to Kufa to have his wounds treated. After recovering, he returned to Medina. His survival was a divine wisdom so that the lineage of Imam Hasan al-Mujtaba (AS) would continue through him and his brother (Zayd).
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Hasan ibn Hasan.
